mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or positive returns occur in fewer than 55% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration that triggers immediate equity sell-offs within the following week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in a single month
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of months where unemployment rises above the 0.3pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand growth that sustains equity valuations over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce a positive 60-day forward return in at least 50% of observed quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a low-inflation environment confirms capacity utilization without pricing pressure, driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of periods where industrial production accelerates while inflation remains below the 0.2% monthly threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls consistently precedes short-term equity rallies as improved employment data reinforces earnings expectations and reduces recession probability.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return direction aligns with payroll growth in less than 55% of monthly observations over a 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of falling federal funds rates and rising unemployment creates a policy accommodation signal that boosts equity risk premiums and generates positive quarterly returns.
failure_condition: Quarters featuring simultaneous rate cuts and unemployment increases yield negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below 1.5% annually triggers recession fears that compress equity multiples and produce negative forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 records positive 20-day returns in over 60% of quarters where annualized GDP growth drops below the 1.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in industrial production during a monetary tightening phase signal supply-side shocks that force immediate equity deleveraging and negative weekly returns.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns fail to materialize in at least 55% of weeks following industrial production declines during tightening periods.
